title: Officers of non-governmental organisations
description: Assist judges in court or by conducting research or preparing legal documents. Assist lawyers by investigating facts, preparing legal documents, or researching legal precedent. Conduct research to support a legal proceeding, to formulate a defense, or to initiate legal action. Search real estate records, examine titles, or summarize pertinent legal or insurance documents or details for a variety of purposes. May compile lists of mortgages, contracts, and other instruments pertaining to titles by searching public and private records for law firms, real estate agencies, or title insurance companies. (Based on 18 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Learn to operate new office technologies as they are developed and implemented.
Schedule and confirm appointments for clients, customers, or supervisors.

Prepare conference or event materials, such as flyers or invitations.

Perform payroll functions, maintain timekeeping information, and process payroll.

Set up and manage paper or electronic filing systems, recording information, updating paperwork, or maintaining documents.
Create, maintain, and enter information into databases.
Use computers for various applications, such as database management or word processing.

Check with employers or other references to verify answers and obtain further information.

Verify the accuracy and completeness of land-related documents accepted for registration and prepare rejection notices when documents are not acceptable.

Examine individual titles to determine if restrictions will affect titles and limit property use.
Copy or summarize recorded documents, such as mortgages and contracts, that affect property titles.
Read search requests to ascertain types of title evidence required and obtain descriptions of properties and names of involved parties.
Conduct private investigations on a paid basis.
Write reports or case summaries to document investigations.
Review complaints and documents filed to determine issues involved or basis for relief.
Review dockets of pending litigation to ensure adequate progress.
Verify that files, complaints, or papers are available and in proper order.
Coordinate judges' meeting and appointment schedules.
Enter information into computerized court calendar, filing, or case management systems.
Maintain judges' law libraries by assembling or updating appropriate documents.
Perform courtroom duties, including calling calendars, administering oaths, and swearing in jury panels and witnesses.
Prepare periodic reports on court proceedings.
Respond to questions from judicial officers or court staff on general legal issues.

Keep abreast of changes in the law and inform judges when cases are affected by these changes.
Prepare affidavits or other legal documents and maintain an organized filing system.
Prepare for trial by organizing exhibits.
Meet with clients and other professionals to discuss case details.
File pleadings with court clerks.
Arbitrate disputes between parties and assist in the real estate closing process.
Attend court sessions to hear oral arguments or record case information.
Communicate with counsel regarding case management or procedural requirements.
Confer with judges concerning legal questions or granting of orders.

Prepare, edit, or review legal documents, including contracts and pleadings.

Enforce courtroom rules of behavior and notify individuals not to disrupt court procedures.
Report need for police or medical assistance to sheriff's office.

Direct and coordinate law office activity, including the delivery of subpoenas.
Gather and analyze research data, such as statutes and legal articles.
Keep and monitor legal volumes to ensure that the law library is up-to-date.
Perform general office duties, such as ordering supplies and maintaining records management database systems.

Open, sort, and distribute incoming correspondence, including faxes and email.
Transcribe recorded proceedings in accordance with established formats.
Greet visitors and determine their access to specific individuals.
Ask speakers to clarify inaudible statements.
Supervise and train other clerical staff.
Record symbols on computer storage media and use transcription to display them as text.

Proofread transcripts for correct spelling of words.

Review operating practices to determine potential improvements.

Record verbatim proceedings using computerized recording equipment or stenomasks.
Take notes in shorthand or use a stenotype machine.
Verify accuracy of transcripts by checking copies against original records of proceedings and accuracy of rulings by checking with judges.
Conduct research, compile data, and prepare papers for consideration and presentation by executives, committees, and boards of directors.
Compile, transcribe, and distribute minutes of meetings; attend meetings to record minutes; prepare meeting agendas, and record and transcribe minutes.
Meet with individuals, special interest groups, and others on behalf of executives, committees, and boards of directors.
Respond to requests during court sessions to read portions of the proceedings already recorded and file a legible transcript of records of a court case with the court clerk's office.
File and store shorthand notes of court sessions, and maintain accurate filing, inventory, mailing, and database systems.
Track the status of requisitions, contracts, and orders, and respond to customer and supplier inquiries about order status, changes, or cancellations.
Prepare, maintain, and review purchasing files, reports, and price lists; approve and pay bills; and compile, copy, sort, and file records of office activities.
Compute, record, and proofread data and other information, such as records or reports, and gather records pertinent to specific problems, reviewing them for completeness and accuracy.
Locate suppliers, using sources such as catalogs and the internet, and contact them to gather information about products needed.
Monitor and direct the work of lower-level clerks or staff, and train or supervise subordinates and other staff members.
Prepare, complete, and mail documents, such as business or government forms and expense reports, as well as correspondence related to various inquiries.
Review files, records, and other documents to obtain information necessary to respond to requests, and verify customer and order information for correctness.
Compose letters in reply to correspondence concerning such items as requests for merchandise, damage claims, and credit information requests.
Gather and summarize pertinent legal or insurance documents or details for a variety of purposes, and assist lawyers by investigating facts and researching legal precedent.

Gather records and attachments for correspondence and legal documents, ensuring all are complete and accurate; prepare documents and correspondence, such as damage claims, credit and billing inquiries, invoices, and service complaints.
